channel.title: FASTER Cycling
channel.itunes:author: FLO Cycling
channel.description: If you're into road cycling, triathlon, gravel cycling, or mountain biking, this podcast will help make you faster. We find industry experts on a wide range of topics to educate you on getting faster.
channel.itunes:keywords: cycling, triathlon, gravel, mountain, road, bikes, bicycle, cyclists, triathlete, ironman

item.itunes:title: The End of the Road
item.title: The End of the Road
item.itunes:summary: It's hard to say but FLO is coming to an end. I discuss all the reasons why and what this means moving forward. Thank you all so much for being a part of this journey. It's meant the world.

item.itunes:title: Improving Pedaling Technique by Reducing Weight on the Handlebars
item.title: Improving Pedaling Technique by Reducing Weight on the Handlebars
item.itunes:summary: If you knew that engaging your hip more when you cycled that you’d get a 10% performance bump, would you be interested? How would you plan to do this? Today, on FASTER we speak with Jim Martin about how simply removing weight from the handlebars will automatically improve your cycling. Listen to this episode to learn more about how to remove weight from the handlebars so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Peptides for Cyclists
item.title: Peptides for Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Peptides are all the rage. Most people are familiar with food grade peptides like collagen but today on FASTER we discuss pharmaceutical peptides. While there are many promising results from the future use of peptides, they continue to be unapproved by the FDA, human trials are limited, they are a banned substance by WADA, and they have the potential to speed the growth of cancer in the body. This episode is covering peptides to try and cut through some of the noise to give you the informatio...
item.description: Peptides are all the rage. Most people are familiar with food grade peptides like collagen but today on FASTER we discuss pharmaceutical peptides. While there are many promising results from the future use of peptides, they continue to be unapproved by the FDA, human trials are limited, they are a banned substance by WADA, and they have the potential to speed the growth of cancer in the body. This episode is covering peptides to try and cut through some of the noise to give you the information you need around peptides. Listen to this episode to learn more about peptides so you can become faster.
item.content:encoded: Peptides are all the rage. Most people are familiar with food grade peptides like collagen but today on FASTER we discuss pharmaceutical peptides. While there are many promising results from the future use of peptides, they continue to be unapproved by the FDA, human trials are limited, they are a banned substance by WADA, and they have the potential to speed the growth of cancer in the body. This episode is covering peptides to try and cut through some of the noise to give you the information you need around peptides. Listen to this episode to learn more about peptides so you can become faster.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Skin Health and Optimization for Faster Cycling
item.title: Skin Health and Optimization for Faster Cycling
item.itunes:summary: At area of roughly 18 square feet, the skin is the bodies largest organ. Today, on FASTER we discuss skin myths and learn how a burn can negatively impact your performance. We also discuss creating a health skin barrier before the race to help hydration and the best kit options based on race conditions. Listen to this episode to optimize your skin so you can become FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Bone Density for Cyclists
item.title: Bone Density for Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: After bone density peaks in our 20s it starts to decline as we age. As cyclist, maintaining bone health is crucial for performance and longevity in the saddle. Today on FASTER, we speak with Cindy Dupuie about bone density and bone health. Women entering menopause will lose 20% of their bone density in the first 8 years and men suffering from low testosterone can experience accelerated bone density loss. Today, there are many options to prevent and slow changes in bone density. We also discus...
item.description: After bone density peaks in our 20s it starts to decline as we age. As cyclist, maintaining bone health is crucial for performance and longevity in the saddle. Today on FASTER, we speak with Cindy Dupuie about bone density and bone health. Women entering menopause will lose 20% of their bone density in the first 8 years and men suffering from low testosterone can experience accelerated bone density loss. Today, there are many options to prevent and slow changes in bone density. We also discuss the myths around calcium and the be all end all for maintaining health bones, supplementation, and hormone replacement therapy. Listen to this episode to maintain strong bones so you can become FASTER and ride longer.
item.content:encoded: After bone density peaks in our 20s it starts to decline as we age. As cyclist, maintaining bone health is crucial for performance and longevity in the saddle. Today on FASTER, we speak with Cindy Dupuie about bone density and bone health. Women entering menopause will lose 20% of their bone density in the first 8 years and men suffering from low testosterone can experience accelerated bone density loss. Today, there are many options to prevent and slow changes in bone density. We also discuss the myths around calcium and the be all end all for maintaining health bones, supplementation, and hormone replacement therapy. Listen to this episode to maintain strong bones so you can become FASTER and ride longer.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: The Impact of Vibration on Muscle Activation, VO2 Max, Heart Rate, and Inflammation for Cyclists
item.title: The Impact of Vibration on Muscle Activation, VO2 Max, Heart Rate, and Inflammation for Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: We've been studying vibration for years and coming up with unique ways to manage it. Some of our initial research is now being supported by further studies. In this episode of FASTER we discuss how vibration leads to increased muscle activation, a higher heart rate, a compromised VO2 Max, and increased inflammation for cyclists when they are not managing it properly. Listen to this episode to learn more about the impacts of vibration on your cycling performance so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Building Muscle for Cyclists and Maximizing Hypertrophy beyond 40
item.title: Building Muscle for Cyclists and Maximizing Hypertrophy beyond 40
item.itunes:summary: The proper body composition for competitive endurance athletes is a fine balance. The too much muscle and you slow down, too little and you are injury prone. In this episode of FASTER we discuss the pros and cons of building muscle for endurance athletes, why we need to manage our muscle composition, isometric and eccentric exercises to maximize hypertrophy, how to fuel when fasting, and how our bodies change after 40. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn how to optimize your muscle comp...
item.description: The proper body composition for competitive endurance athletes is a fine balance. The too much muscle and you slow down, too little and you are injury prone. In this episode of FASTER we discuss the pros and cons of building muscle for endurance athletes, why we need to manage our muscle composition, isometric and eccentric exercises to maximize hypertrophy, how to fuel when fasting, and how our bodies change after 40. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn how to optimize your muscle composition so you can become faster.
item.content:encoded: The proper body composition for competitive endurance athletes is a fine balance. The too much muscle and you slow down, too little and you are injury prone. In this episode of FASTER we discuss the pros and cons of building muscle for endurance athletes, why we need to manage our muscle composition, isometric and eccentric exercises to maximize hypertrophy, how to fuel when fasting, and how our bodies change after 40. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn how to optimize your muscle composition so you can become faster.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: How Alcohol Impacts Endurance Sport Performance
item.title: How Alcohol Impacts Endurance Sport Performance
item.itunes:summary: Consuming alcohol is something most of the adult population takes part in. For years, a drink of wine was considered healthy but these days there is not evidence that alcohol is positive in any way. Today, on FASTER we look beyond the negative impact alcohol has on our health and explore how it impacts our performance as athletes. Even a drink a week can impact performance if consumed at the wrong time. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn more about alcohol so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: The Ultimate Supplement Stack for Cyclists and Triathletes
item.title: The Ultimate Supplement Stack for Cyclists and Triathletes
item.itunes:summary: It's easy to get overwhelmed with the number of options when it comes to supplements. Even when you have a clue what you're looking for you're faced with finding a supplement that is a high quality product. The supplementation industry lacks the standards that deliver a quality product in all cases. On this episode of FASTER we interview Jeff Byers from Momentous and discuss the ideal supplement stack for cyclists and triathletes. Momentous is brand partner of ours on the FLO Factory Team and...
item.description: It's easy to get overwhelmed with the number of options when it comes to supplements. Even when you have a clue what you're looking for you're faced with finding a supplement that is a high quality product. The supplementation industry lacks the standards that deliver a quality product in all cases. On this episode of FASTER we interview Jeff Byers from Momentous and discuss the ideal supplement stack for cyclists and triathletes. Momentous is brand partner of ours on the FLO Factory Team and I personally love their products. They've built a business around providing the best quality products tailored to athletes. It's why they supply all the NFL teams and much of the endurance sports community. Listen to this episode to learn what the ideal supplement stack is so you can become FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Respiratory Muscle Training for Faster Cycling and better VO2 Max
item.title: Respiratory Muscle Training for Faster Cycling and better VO2 Max
item.itunes:summary: The lungs you are born with do not change like a muscle when exercised. For this reason, many people believe there isn't much that can be done to improve breathing for performance. I was fascinated to find out that while you can't change the lungs you have you can change how you use the lungs you have. In this episode of FASTER we welcome back Dr. Andrew Sellars and introduce Luke Way from Isocapnic. They've developed a respiratory muscle trainer that simulates your lungs at a increased effor...
item.description: The lungs you are born with do not change like a muscle when exercised. For this reason, many people believe there isn't much that can be done to improve breathing for performance. I was fascinated to find out that while you can't change the lungs you have you can change how you use the lungs you have. In this episode of FASTER we welcome back Dr. Andrew Sellars and introduce Luke Way from Isocapnic. They've developed a respiratory muscle trainer that simulates your lungs at a increased effort. Normally, when rested, deep breathes will leave you lightheaded, but by intelligently regulating the CO2 that exits the body they are able to train your lungs while sitting on the couch. Listen to this episode to learn more about respiratory muscle training so you can become faster and increase your VO2 Max.
item.content:encoded: The lungs you are born with do not change like a muscle when exercised. For this reason, many people believe there isn't much that can be done to improve breathing for performance. I was fascinated to find out that while you can't change the lungs you have you can change how you use the lungs you have. In this episode of FASTER we welcome back Dr. Andrew Sellars and introduce Luke Way from Isocapnic. They've developed a respiratory muscle trainer that simulates your lungs at a increased effort. Normally, when rested, deep breathes will leave you lightheaded, but by intelligently regulating the CO2 that exits the body they are able to train your lungs while sitting on the couch. Listen to this episode to learn more about respiratory muscle training so you can become faster and increase your VO2 Max.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Matching Breathing Rate to Cycling Cadence and a VO2Max Masterclass
item.title: Matching Breathing Rate to Cycling Cadence and a VO2Max Masterclass
item.itunes:summary: After a recent podcast with Jim Martin we discussed the idea of matching breathing rate to cycling cadence and how it creates more efficient cycling. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Dr. Andrew Sellars about breathing rate and cycling/running cadence. It's one of my favorite episodes in a long time and I highly recommend listening. Andrew's knowledge as a coach, his background in physiology and muscle engagement in the pedal stroke along with his deep knowledge in human breathing make f...
item.description: After a recent podcast with Jim Martin we discussed the idea of matching breathing rate to cycling cadence and how it creates more efficient cycling. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Dr. Andrew Sellars about breathing rate and cycling/running cadence. It's one of my favorite episodes in a long time and I highly recommend listening. Andrew's knowledge as a coach, his background in physiology and muscle engagement in the pedal stroke along with his deep knowledge in human breathing make for an episode that will surprise you. Listen to this episode to improve your breathing so you can become faster.
item.content:encoded: After a recent podcast with Jim Martin we discussed the idea of matching breathing rate to cycling cadence and how it creates more efficient cycling. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Dr. Andrew Sellars about breathing rate and cycling/running cadence. It's one of my favorite episodes in a long time and I highly recommend listening. Andrew's knowledge as a coach, his background in physiology and muscle engagement in the pedal stroke along with his deep knowledge in human breathing make for an episode that will surprise you. Listen to this episode to improve your breathing so you can become faster.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Adaptive Training for Cyclists with JOIN
item.title: Adaptive Training for Cyclists with JOIN
item.itunes:summary: Instead of picking a training methodology like threshold training or a polarized approach, what if you created a training that was adaptive? Wouldn't this be a better solution for you as an athlete in the long run? Today, on FASTER we are joined by Jim Van Der Berg who is the creator of JOIN Cycling, an ex-semi pro cyclist the the current coach of many pro cyclists. Jim also has a degree in exercise physiology and sports psychology. Jim has taken in extensive background in cyclint and created...
item.description:
Instead of picking a training methodology like threshold training or a polarized approach, what if you created a training that was adaptive? Wouldn't this be a better solution for you as an athlete in the long run? Today, on FASTER we are joined by Jim Van Der Berg who is the creator of JOIN Cycling, an ex-semi pro cyclist the the current coach of many pro cyclists. Jim also has a degree in exercise physiology and sports psychology. Jim has taken in extensive background in cyclint and created a training app that delivers workouts in an adaptive way. Instead of a training plan JOIN is a training tool. We discuss training at a high-level and uncover the magic behind how JOIN works. Listen to this episode to learn how adaptive training and make you FASTER. 

Watt Points: 30

30 Day Free Trial of JOIN
item.content:encoded:
Instead of picking a training methodology like threshold training or a polarized approach, what if you created a training that was adaptive? Wouldn't this be a better solution for you as an athlete in the long run? Today, on FASTER we are joined by Jim Van Der Berg who is the creator of JOIN Cycling, an ex-semi pro cyclist the the current coach of many pro cyclists. Jim also has a degree in exercise physiology and sports psychology. Jim has taken in extensive background in cyclint and created a training app that delivers workouts in an adaptive way. Instead of a training plan JOIN is a training tool. We discuss training at a high-level and uncover the magic behind how JOIN works. Listen to this episode to learn how adaptive training and make you FASTER. 

Watt Points: 30

30 Day Free Trial of JOIN

item.itunes:title: TrainingPeaks Virtual and Making Virtual Cycling More Realistic
item.title: TrainingPeaks Virtual and Making Virtual Cycling More Realistic
item.itunes:summary: If you've ever ridden on a virtual cycling platform you may immediately notice that it's not representative of real-world cycling. When you come out of a corner on the road you have to add watts to the pedals to speed back up. Most virtual training platforms end up acting more like a spin class. George Gilbert set out to correct this process and founded IndieVelo in 2022. A short two years later his company was acquired by TrainingPeaks and the product has been renamed TrainingPeaks Virtual. ...
item.description: If you've ever ridden on a virtual cycling platform you may immediately notice that it's not representative of real-world cycling. When you come out of a corner on the road you have to add watts to the pedals to speed back up. Most virtual training platforms end up acting more like a spin class. George Gilbert set out to correct this process and founded IndieVelo in 2022. A short two years later his company was acquired by TrainingPeaks and the product has been renamed TrainingPeaks Virtual. Listen to this episode to learn how adding real-world physics to your training platform can make you a FASTER cyclist when you get back on the road.
item.content:encoded: If you've ever ridden on a virtual cycling platform you may immediately notice that it's not representative of real-world cycling. When you come out of a corner on the road you have to add watts to the pedals to speed back up. Most virtual training platforms end up acting more like a spin class. George Gilbert set out to correct this process and founded IndieVelo in 2022. A short two years later his company was acquired by TrainingPeaks and the product has been renamed TrainingPeaks Virtual. Listen to this episode to learn how adding real-world physics to your training platform can make you a FASTER cyclist when you get back on the road.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Mastering Electrolytes for Cyclists
item.title: Mastering Electrolytes for Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: We welcome back the legendary Scott Tindal to discuss electrolytes for cyclists. Today, electrolytes have become common for daily use even amongst non-endurance athletes. Many of the electrolyte products contain ingredients that are not needed. In this episode of FASTER we break down what electrolytes are, what electrolytes you need, how electrolytes help cyclists, and how to optimize your electrolyte intake so you can become FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Mastering Race Across America RAAM
item.title: Mastering Race Across America RAAM
item.itunes:summary: RAAM or Race Across America is a legendary race with cyclists racing across the country. Traditionally a solo race, it has expanded to include 2, 4, and 8-person teams. When you think of riding across the country you may think it would take 30 days. However, the fastest solo time is now less than 8 days. Today on FASTER we are joined by Rick Boethling who manages the day-to-day operations at RAAM and has crewed the race for his father many times. Rick shares his secrets for conquering the rac...
item.description: RAAM or Race Across America is a legendary race with cyclists racing across the country. Traditionally a solo race, it has expanded to include 2, 4, and 8-person teams. When you think of riding across the country you may think it would take 30 days. However, the fastest solo time is now less than 8 days. Today on FASTER we are joined by Rick Boethling who manages the day-to-day operations at RAAM and has crewed the race for his father many times. Rick shares his secrets for conquering the race and performing at your best. Listen to this episode to learn how to pick the perfect support crew and optimize your race plan so you can be FASTER racing across America.
item.content:encoded: RAAM or Race Across America is a legendary race with cyclists racing across the country. Traditionally a solo race, it has expanded to include 2, 4, and 8-person teams. When you think of riding across the country you may think it would take 30 days. However, the fastest solo time is now less than 8 days. Today on FASTER we are joined by Rick Boethling who manages the day-to-day operations at RAAM and has crewed the race for his father many times. Rick shares his secrets for conquering the race and performing at your best. Listen to this episode to learn how to pick the perfect support crew and optimize your race plan so you can be FASTER racing across America.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Hormone Replacement, Imbalance, and Optimization for Cyclists
item.title: Hormone Replacement, Imbalance, and Optimization for Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: On this episode of FASTER Cycling we talk with Dr. Sharon Byun and Dr. Lawrence Yeung about hormones for men and women. We discuss hormone replacement therapy, signs and myths around hormone imbalances, how to optimize hormones for cyclists, and much more. Finally we discuss new work in hormones around menopause and Dr. Mary Claire Haver's work. Listen to this episode to optimize your hormones so you can be FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Fueling Before and After Training for Endurance Athletes
item.title: Fueling Before and After Training for Endurance Athletes
item.itunes:summary: We've had several episodes discussing fueling during training and racing but today we cover fueling before and after training and racing. Sam Shepherd from Precision Hydration covers pre-training and pre-race strategies, including carb loading, carb sources, tips for fasted athletes and why fueling post activity is best looked at as part of you training session not a separate event. Listen to this episode to lock in your nutrition around training and racing so you can become FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Cadence for Cyclist & 2x vs 1x Drive Trains
item.title: Cadence for Cyclist & 2x vs 1x Drive Trains
item.itunes:summary: In this episode of FASTER we have Dr. Jim Martin back to discuss cadence for cyclists. We also discuss the main differences between 2x and 1x drive trains and why 2x is likely faster for most athletes. We cover a wide range of topics including metabolic cost, cost of unloaded cycling, weave frequency, picking the right cassette and much more. Listen to this episode to learn how to optimize your cadence so you can become FASTER. Watt Points: 25

item.itunes:title: Understanding Body Composition for Cyclists and Triathletes
item.title: Understanding Body Composition for Cyclists and Triathletes
item.itunes:summary: Body composition is hot topic amongst cyclists and triathletes. While it's common to want to achieve a specific watts/kg, getting there in a healthy way is crucial. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Alex Larson about body composition and how each athlete is unique. Listen to this episode to learn all about body composition you can become FASTER.

item.itunes:title: Using Visualization to Improve Sports Performance
item.title: Using Visualization to Improve Sports Performance
item.itunes:summary: Many world class athletes can be seen with their eyes closed before a major event. I often wondered what they were doing and I've learned that most are visualizing. Visualization is a sports psychology technique that is proven to improve sports performance. In this episode of FASTER we discuss confidence and trust which are the building blocks of visualization and then discuss everything about visualization. Listen to this episode to learn how to use visualization to make you a FASTER athlete.

item.content:encoded: Many world class athletes can be seen with their eyes closed before a major event. I often wondered what they were doing and I've learned that most are visualizing. Visualization is a sports psychology technique that is proven to improve sports performance. In this episode of FASTER we discuss confidence and trust which are the building blocks of visualization and then discuss everything about visualization. Listen to this episode to learn how to use visualization to make you a FASTER athlete.

item.itunes:summary: Most people in the west think of two things when discussing mushrooms. Food and hallucinogens. However, mushrooms have been used in the east for thousands of years for health. Recently, a cycling team used cordyceps mushrooms to help their athletes improve VO2 Max and studies show humans do get a VO2 Max boost from mushrooms. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Danielle Ryan Dawson from FourSigmatic about how adaptogens and mushrooms can be used to improve sports performance. Listen to thi...
item.description:
Most people in the west think of two things when discussing mushrooms. Food and hallucinogens. However, mushrooms have been used in the east for thousands of years for health. Recently, a cycling team used cordyceps mushrooms to help their athletes improve VO2 Max and studies show humans do get a VO2 Max boost from mushrooms. In this episode of FASTER we talk with Danielle Ryan Dawson from FourSigmatic about how adaptogens and mushrooms can be used to improve sports performance. Listen to this episode to learn more about mushroom so you can be faster.

Watt Points: 45-60

item.itunes:title: Gravel Wheels are Faster Than Road Wheels and Dollars/Watt
item.title: Gravel Wheels are Faster Than Road Wheels and Dollars/Watt
item.itunes:summary: Did you know that the fastest disc brake wheels we make are our gravel wheels even through they are 23mm shallower than our deepest road wheel? In this episode we discuss why and have a detailed conversation about Dollars/Watt when looking at gear. Most people look at the cost and the number of watts saved but time needs to be considered. We break down the math and show you how to save the most watts for the least amount of money. Watts/Dollar Blog Post

item.title: Managing Pain for Cyclists to Ensure Longevity In the Saddle
item.itunes:summary: As we age pain can increase during physical exercise. However, pain is not always a sign to stop. It may mean we need to push through or find new ways to keep moving. Old theory states that as we age we must slow down but new research proves that the more we move the longer we live and the better quality of life we have. In this episode of FASTER Dr. Kyle Buth from Crafted Wellness discusses using pain as an indicator to keep moving forward as we age to ensure we can stay active and health do...
item.description:
As we age pain can increase during physical exercise. However, pain is not always a sign to stop. It may mean we need to push through or find new ways to keep moving. Old theory states that as we age we must slow down but new research proves that the more we move the longer we live and the better quality of life we have. In this episode of FASTER Dr. Kyle Buth from Crafted Wellness discusses using pain as an indicator to keep moving forward as we age to ensure we can stay active and health doing the things we love. Listen to this episode to learn how to use pain to be FASTER.

Watt Points: 0-100%

item.description:
Have you ever considered how your spine alignment during sleep impacts your performance? We'll Dr. Martone has created a whole career on helping people improve the position of their spine during sleep to improve performance and prevent injury. Dr. Martone a practicing chiropractor and mountain biker herniated a disc which had him re-evaluate everything he knew and was teaching his patients. This led to a complete change in his chiropractor practice and led to the creation of his company Neck Nest. Now Dr. Martone is faster on the bike without pain. Listen to this episode to learn how the alignment of your spine during sleep can help make you FASTER.

Watt Points: 30

item.itunes:title: Braking Efficiently To Save Watts & Preserve Input Power
item.title: Braking Efficiently To Save Watts & Preserve Input Power
item.itunes:summary: Braking has the potential to not only save watts expended but can also save more power in your legs. It's been calculated that 25% of the watts put into the pedals are burned in braking. When you learn to brake more efficiently you waste fewer watts during braking and can use the brakes less often when cornering saving power in your legs. This week on FASTER we talk with Dr. Matt Miller about breaking and his compnay Brake Ace. Matt literally has a PHD in braking. Listen to this episode to le...
item.description:
Braking has the potential to not only save watts expended but can also save more power in your legs. It's been calculated that 25% of the watts put into the pedals are burned in braking. When you learn to brake more efficiently you waste fewer watts during braking and can use the brakes less often when cornering saving power in your legs. This week on FASTER we talk with Dr. Matt Miller about breaking and his compnay Brake Ace. Matt literally has a PHD in braking. Listen to this episode to learn about braking an brake technique so you can become faster. Some times you need to slow down to speed up.

Watt Points: 5-10

item.title: Focused Strength Training, Nutrition, and Recovery for Cyclist with Dialed Health
item.itunes:summary: Strength training for cyclists needs to be specific to the cyclist and their discipline. Derek Teel from Dialed Health offers dozens of workouts and programs for all disciplines of cycling. With options for people with no equipment to more advanced programs using a variety of tools including, foam rollers, kettlebells, TRX, bands, free weights, boxes and much more there is something for everyone. Derek comes racing background and personal training background have placed him in a unique positi...
item.description:
Strength training for cyclists needs to be specific to the cyclist and their discipline. Derek Teel from Dialed Health offers dozens of workouts and programs for all disciplines of cycling. With options for people with no equipment to more advanced programs using a variety of tools including, foam rollers, kettlebells, TRX, bands, free weights, boxes and much more there is something for everyone. Derek comes racing background and personal training background have placed him in a unique position to teach cyclists how and when to add strength training to their workout schedule. Derek also focuses on nutrition, injury, and recovery preparing you as a cyclist to make your time on the bike productive. Listen to this episode to learn more about preparing your body for the bike so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 15-25

item.title: Introducing the FLO Aid Bag + Five More Product
item.itunes:summary: It's not a question of if something happens on the bike, it's a matter of when. Over the years we've seen many people go down on the bike resulting in minor bumps and bruises to serious injury of death. We've also seen many people stranded on the side of the road with a mechanical issue. The FLO Aid Bag is the first bag of it's kind to include both Bike Aid and First Aid in the same bag. We started developing this product more than a year ago an in the process developed five new products to m...
item.description: It's not a question of if something happens on the bike, it's a matter of when. Over the years we've seen many people go down on the bike resulting in minor bumps and bruises to serious injury of death. We've also seen many people stranded on the side of the road with a mechanical issue. The FLO Aid Bag is the first bag of it's kind to include both Bike Aid and First Aid in the same bag. We started developing this product more than a year ago an in the process developed five new products to make the FLO Aid Bag what it is. The FLO Aid Bag is perfect for any ride or in transition. Listen to this episode to learn all about the new FLO Aid Bag, the tools we designed along the way, and our cycling specific first aid so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Wheel Balance, Gaining Speed, Reducing Vibration, and Eliminating Speed Wobbles
item.title: Wheel Balance, Gaining Speed, Reducing Vibration, and Eliminating Speed Wobbles
item.itunes:summary: Wheel balance is an important aspect of cycling that affects the overall performance, comfort, and safety of the ride. When a wheel is out of balance, it causes vibrations, reduces stability, wastes watts, and potentially leads to premature wear of components such as tires and bearings. The truth is, cycling wheels are normally out of balance. However, most people never realize they are. This week on FASTER we discuss what causes wheel imbalance and how to properly balance a wheel so you can ...
item.description:
Wheel balance is an important aspect of cycling that affects the overall performance, comfort, and safety of the ride. When a wheel is out of balance, it causes vibrations, reduces stability, wastes watts, and potentially leads to premature wear of components such as tires and bearings. The truth is, cycling wheels are normally out of balance. However, most people never realize they are. This week on FASTER we discuss what causes wheel imbalance and how to properly balance a wheel so you can become FASTER.

Watt Points: 10

Golf Weights
Loctite Fun-Tak

item.itunes:title: Understanding Spokes, Deformation, Fatigue, and Creep
item.title: Understanding Spokes, Deformation, Fatigue, and Creep
item.itunes:summary: Spokes are often one of the most overlooked but important components of a cycling wheel. Most people think spokes are all the same but this could not be further from the truth. On this episode of FASTER we discuss why we use Sapim CX-Ray spokes for all of our wheels and why understanding elastic and plastic deformation, fatigue, and creep will help you make the best choices for spokes and wheels. Listen to this episode to learn about the mechanical properties of spokes and how picking the rig...
item.description:
Spokes are often one of the most overlooked but important components of a cycling wheel. Most people think spokes are all the same but this could not be further from the truth. On this episode of FASTER we discuss why we use Sapim CX-Ray spokes for all of our wheels and why understanding elastic and plastic deformation, fatigue, and creep will help you make the best choices for spokes and wheels. Listen to this episode to learn about the mechanical properties of spokes and how picking the right spokes will keep you riding and make you faster.

Watt Points: 5

item.itunes:title: The Engineering Behind the FLO Locking Chuck
item.title: The Engineering Behind the FLO Locking Chuck
item.itunes:summary: The FLO Locking Chuck was released earlier this year as a pre-order and has recently started shipping. The engineering that went into the FLO Locking Chuck allows the user to bleed air pressure when used with the FLO Air Gauge. The two make the perfect combo to set accurate pressure when at a race or on the go. Listen to this episode to learn how we designed the FLO Locking Chuck to allow for pressure bleeding and how the dial advances locking chucks from digital control to analog control.&nb...

item.itunes:title: Introducing the FLO Mini Pump
item.title: Introducing the FLO Mini Pump
item.itunes:summary: Today, we are releasing a new product the FLO Mini Pump. As an addition to our modular inflation line, the FLO Mini Pump features a full aluminum body, extendible hose, is compatible with Presta, Schrader, and Dunlop valves, and has a locking chuck made from aluminum. The white powder coat finish gives a stylish look to the FLO Mini Pump. The pump is rated to up 120 psi to match our FLO Air Gauge and the two make the perfect pump system for your next race or while you travel. For every watt y...
item.description: Today, we are releasing a new product the FLO Mini Pump. As an addition to our modular inflation line, the FLO Mini Pump features a full aluminum body, extendible hose, is compatible with Presta, Schrader, and Dunlop valves, and has a locking chuck made from aluminum. The white powder coat finish gives a stylish look to the FLO Mini Pump. The pump is rated to up 120 psi to match our FLO Air Gauge and the two make the perfect pump system for your next race or while you travel. For every watt you over inflate your tires you are losing 1 watt to rolling resistance. Set accurate pressure with the FLO Mini Pump and FLO Air Gauge.
item.content:encoded: Today, we are releasing a new product the FLO Mini Pump. As an addition to our modular inflation line, the FLO Mini Pump features a full aluminum body, extendible hose, is compatible with Presta, Schrader, and Dunlop valves, and has a locking chuck made from aluminum. The white powder coat finish gives a stylish look to the FLO Mini Pump. The pump is rated to up 120 psi to match our FLO Air Gauge and the two make the perfect pump system for your next race or while you travel. For every watt you over inflate your tires you are losing 1 watt to rolling resistance. Set accurate pressure with the FLO Mini Pump and FLO Air Gauge.
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: IM Hawaii 70.3 and IM Kona Wheel, Tire, and Tire Pressure Selection
item.title: IM Hawaii 70.3 and IM Kona Wheel, Tire, and Tire Pressure Selection
item.itunes:summary: Kona is one of the most iconic courses of Ironman. IM Hawaii 70.3 finds most of its bike course on the Kona course. The winds make it illegal to use a disc wheel, the heat is extremely high, and the roads are loaded with steel belted radial. After travelling to the race for seven years I've learned a lot about the course and how to prepare yourself. On this episode of FASTER we discuss wheels, tires and tire pressure and how selecting the right options can save you more than 10 minutes on the...
item.description:
Kona is one of the most iconic courses of Ironman. IM Hawaii 70.3 finds most of its bike course on the Kona course. The winds make it illegal to use a disc wheel, the heat is extremely high, and the roads are loaded with steel belted radial. After travelling to the race for seven years I've learned a lot about the course and how to prepare yourself. On this episode of FASTER we discuss wheels, tires and tire pressure and how selecting the right options can save you more than 10 minutes on the course.

Watt Points: 40

After travelling to the race for seven years I've learned a lot about the course and how to prepare yourself. On this episode of FASTER we discuss wheels, tires and tire pressure and how selecting the right options can save you more than 10 minutes on the course.

item.itunes:title: Using Bike Tires as Shocks on Gravel Terrain and Rough Roads
item.title: Using Bike Tires as Shocks on Gravel Terrain and Rough Roads
item.itunes:summary: While there was a debate for many years about what a gravel bike was, the standard gravel bike does not have shocks. For those struggling with bike handling on gravel or getting beat up on rough roads, managing your tire size and pressure is the best option for create a "shock" for bikes without shocks. Many people look to the frame or other options to reduce vibration but the best bang for the buck is in the tires. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn about using bikes tires as shocks s...
item.description:
While there was a debate for many years about what a gravel bike was, the standard gravel bike does not have shocks. For those struggling with bike handling on gravel or getting beat up on rough roads, managing your tire size and pressure is the best option for create a "shock" for bikes without shocks. Many people look to the frame or other options to reduce vibration but the best bang for the buck is in the tires. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn about using bikes tires as shocks so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 15

Many people look to the frame or other options to reduce vibration but the best bang for the buck is in the tires. Listen to this episode of FASTER to learn about using bikes tires as shocks so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Why Wider Tires and Rims Require Lower Tire Pressure
item.title: Why Wider Tires and Rims Require Lower Tire Pressure
item.itunes:summary: You've likely heard that it's best to lower your tire pressure for wider tires and rims. The truth is it's required. When tires and rims get wider high tire pressures can become dangerous. In this episode of FASTER we talk through the why so you understand the science behind lower tire pressure for wider rims and tires. Watt Points: 20

item.title: Understanding How Carbon Rims Absorb Vibration and Make You Faster
item.itunes:summary: Vibration plays a key role in athletic performance. From the ability to handle a bike to fatigue in the human body, minimizing vibration is key. Today on FASTER, we discuss why carbon fiber rims are the ideal choice for reducing vibration on the road when compared to their aluminum counterparts. Listen to this episode to learn how to better manage your vibration so you can become FASTER. Watt Points: 3

item.itunes:summary: The Belgian Waffle Ride in San Marcos, CA is known for every changing course which blends road and very technical unroad terrain. In order to have your best day in the Belgian Waffle Ride gear selection is vitally important. In this episode of FASTER, we explore the course and talk about wheel & tire selection, tire pressure, aerodynamics, bike handling skills and more. Listen to this episode to help set you up to be the fastest on race day at the Belgian Waffle Ride. Watt Points: 20

item.itunes:title: Gravel Bike Handling Masterclass with Lee McCormack
item.title: Gravel Bike Handling Masterclass with Lee McCormack
item.itunes:summary: The importance of bike handling changes as the terrain changes. For triathletes and road cyclists, the bike handling skills are on the lower end whereas mountain biking is on the higher end. With the explosion of gravel cycling, we see many triathletes and road cyclists moving to gravel cycling and often bike handling skills can be a limiting factor for an athlete's success. Today, on Faster we talk with Lee McCormack for a masterclass on bike handling skills so you can learn to ride confiden...
item.description:
The importance of bike handling changes as the terrain changes. For triathletes and road cyclists, the bike handling skills are on the lower end whereas mountain biking is on the higher end. With the explosion of gravel cycling, we see many triathletes and road cyclists moving to gravel cycling and often bike handling skills can be a limiting factor for an athlete's success. Today, on Faster we talk with Lee McCormack for a masterclass on bike handling skills so you can learn to ride confidently and become a faster gravel cyclist.

Watt Points: 90-100

In this episode of Faster we discuss myths around wheel selection for coastal courses and help you pick your best wheels. We also discuss setting tire pressure on courses with sections of rough road and offer our advice on the ideal tire for this course. Listen to this episode to learn how to get set up for IM Oceanside or similar courses so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Introducing the FLO Air Gage - Set Accurate Tire Pressures
item.title: Introducing the FLO Air Gage - Set Accurate Tire Pressures
item.itunes:summary: Over inflating your tires may seem like a trivial issue but today we know that for every 1 psi you over inflate your tires you lose roughly 1 watt to rolling resistance. We also know that most standard floor pumps are only accurate to +/- 9 psi which means that a cyclist with a 200 watt FTP could be giving up close to 5% of their power to an inaccurate pump. It’s why we created a new product called the FLO Air Gage. This episode discusses, the FLO Air Gage and how it came to be. Learn more ab...
item.description:
Over inflating your tires may seem like a trivial issue but today we know that for every 1 psi you over inflate your tires you lose roughly 1 watt to rolling resistance. We also know that most standard floor pumps are only accurate to +/- 9 psi which means that a cyclist with a 200 watt FTP could be giving up close to 5% of their power to an inaccurate pump. It’s why we created a new product called the FLO Air Gage. This episode discusses, the FLO Air Gage and how it came to be. Learn more about the FLO Air Gage and setting accurate tire pressures so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 9 Watts

Impedance and Rolling Resistance Episode
item.content:encoded:
Over inflating your tires may seem like a trivial issue but today we know that for every 1 psi you over inflate your tires you lose roughly 1 watt to rolling resistance. We also know that most standard floor pumps are only accurate to +/- 9 psi which means that a cyclist with a 200 watt FTP could be giving up close to 5% of their power to an inaccurate pump. It’s why we created a new product called the FLO Air Gage. This episode discusses, the FLO Air Gage and how it came to be. Learn more about the FLO Air Gage and setting accurate tire pressures so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 9 Watts

Impedance and Rolling Resistance Episode
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.title: Life, Family, Work, and Training Balance for Endurance Athletes
item.itunes:summary: Life balance for most triathletes is a constant challenge. Throw in the last couple years over COVID and the return to our new lives and it’s been nearly impossible. Today, on FASTER we welcome back Max Jones to discuss his journey from owner at Elevate Sports Performance and a competitive triathlete, to leaving for many new careers, starting a family, losing a house in a fire, becoming a full time runner and back to a triathlete on the FLO Factory Team. Learn how he’s used his strength and c...
item.description:
Life balance for most triathletes is a constant challenge. Throw in the last couple years over COVID and the return to our new lives and it’s been nearly impossible. Today, on FASTER we welcome back Max Jones to discuss his journey from owner at Elevate Sports Performance and a competitive triathlete, to leaving for many new careers, starting a family, losing a house in a fire, becoming a full time runner and back to a triathlete on the FLO Factory Team. Learn how he’s used his strength and conditioning background to make a comeback all while finding balance in a very busy, very wonderful life.

Watt Points: 30

item.itunes:summary: Ketones are naturally produced in the body when you enter ketosis. This happens when the body is burning fat as its primary fuel source. There’s been lots of discussion on ketosis for diet but what about performance athletes. Today on Faster we talk with Frank Llosa from KetoneAid who is making a Ketone Ester for performance athletes and Anthony Kunkel who uses KetoneAid and lives a life in Ketosis while being a two-time national champion ultra distance runner. Listen to this episode to chall...
item.description:
Ketones are naturally produced in the body when you enter ketosis. This happens when the body is burning fat as its primary fuel source. There’s been lots of discussion on ketosis for diet but what about performance athletes. Today on Faster we talk with Frank Llosa from KetoneAid who is making a Ketone Ester for performance athletes and Anthony Kunkel who uses KetoneAid and lives a life in Ketosis while being a two-time national champion ultra distance runner. Listen to this episode to challenge your belief on using carbs as a fuel source and to learn how to use Ketones to become faster.

In exciting news FASTER Cycling has been listed in the 45 Best Cycling Podcasts by FeedSpot. Check out the list here.

Watt Points: 10-30

item.itunes:title: FuelIn, Carb Training, Keto, & Mouth Rinsing For Cyclists
item.title: FuelIn, Carb Training, Keto, & Mouth Rinsing For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: FatMax is a popular topic amongst endurance athletes. While it’s a great number to improve, there’s a false belief forming that carbs are the enemy. The truth is, in order to perform at our highest level, we need to fuel our bodies with carbohydrates. The brilliant Scott Tindal from FuelIn joins us again for a fascinating discussion on training your body to use carbohydrates for your race. Along the way we cover lots of details on carbs vs. fat, carb loading, and the types of carbs to consume...
item.description:
FatMax is a popular topic amongst endurance athletes. While it’s a great number to improve, there’s a false belief forming that carbs are the enemy. The truth is, in order to perform at our highest level, we need to fuel our bodies with carbohydrates. The brilliant Scott Tindal from FuelIn joins us again for a fascinating discussion on training your body to use carbohydrates for your race. Along the way we cover lots of details on carbs vs. fat, carb loading, and the types of carbs to consume. Listen to this episode to learn how to use carbs to your advantage so you can become faster.

Watt Points: Save yourself from going to 0 watts.

item.itunes:title: Aerotune, CdA, VO2 Max, VLamax, & Metabolic Testing For Cyclists
item.title: Aerotune, CdA, VO2 Max, VLamax, & Metabolic Testing For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Training and aerodynamics are two the largest watt savings topics we hear about on FASTER. Today on FASTER we are talking with Sebastian Schluricke and Bjorn Kafka from Aerotune who are optimizing aerodynamics and performing metabolic testing in a web based software. On the aero side they allow you to upload ride data to determine your CdA. On the training side they’ve developed a user friendly metabolic testing protocol that gives the athlete tools to optimize both VO2 Max and VLamax. Later ...
item.description:
Training and aerodynamics are two the largest watt savings topics we hear about on FASTER. Today on FASTER we are talking with Sebastian Schluricke and Bjorn Kafka from Aerotune who are optimizing aerodynamics and performing metabolic testing in a web based software. On the aero side they allow you to upload ride data to determine your CdA. On the training side they’ve developed a user friendly metabolic testing protocol that gives the athlete tools to optimize both VO2 Max and VLamax. Later this year they will be releasing custom training plans based on your metabolic test results which will simplify a very complex field for the everyday athlete. Today’s show is broken into two halves. We talk about aero in the beginning and metabolic testing in the second half. Bjorn has a way of explaining metabolic testing like I’ve never heard before and my understanding is much clearer. If you’ve wanted to learn more about metabolic testing and why it’s important for you as an athlete, this is a must listen to episode. Listen to this episode to optimize your aerodynamic position and your metabolic efficiency so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 70-80
item.content:encoded:
Training and aerodynamics are two the largest watt savings topics we hear about on FASTER. Today on FASTER we are talking with Sebastian Schluricke and Bjorn Kafka from Aerotune who are optimizing aerodynamics and performing metabolic testing in a web based software. On the aero side they allow you to upload ride data to determine your CdA. On the training side they’ve developed a user friendly metabolic testing protocol that gives the athlete tools to optimize both VO2 Max and VLamax. Later this year they will be releasing custom training plans based on your metabolic test results which will simplify a very complex field for the everyday athlete. Today’s show is broken into two halves. We talk about aero in the beginning and metabolic testing in the second half. Bjorn has a way of explaining metabolic testing like I’ve never heard before and my understanding is much clearer. If you’ve wanted to learn more about metabolic testing and why it’s important for you as an athlete, this is a must listen to episode. Listen to this episode to optimize your aerodynamic position and your metabolic efficiency so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 70-80
item.itunes:author: FLO Cycling

item.itunes:title: Breakaway, Data Analysis, Motivation, & Workout Recommendations For Cyclists
item.title: Breakaway, Data Analysis, Motivation, & Workout Recommendations For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: What do you do with all of the data that you collect while riding your bike? Most cyclist upload it to Strava where they compare themselves to the leaderboard. In a lot of cases this can be demotivating. The Breakaway is using your data to help you compare yourself to yourself. They’re analyzing your power data and providing actionable next steps to help you improve. You’re given a ranking based on your age and sex so you can see where you stand with people that are relative to you. They also...
item.description:
What do you do with all of the data that you collect while riding your bike? Most cyclist upload it to Strava where they compare themselves to the leaderboard. In a lot of cases this can be demotivating. The Breakaway is using your data to help you compare yourself to yourself. They’re analyzing your power data and providing actionable next steps to help you improve. You’re given a ranking based on your age and sex so you can see where you stand with people that are relative to you. They also have an eight week rolling average on data analysis so you aren’t continually comparing yourself to what you could do in the past. The goal is to motivate you to be your best today. With integrations to most major platforms and workout recommendations, Breakaway is meant to be your cycling fitness dashboard when you’re not riding. Listen to this episode to learn how to use your data and results to motivate yourself so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 30

Historically a lot of people have thought of meditation as a spiritual or religious practice, but that is starting to change. Today on Faster we welcome back Nick Salazar to talk about meditation and his new company Stillside after making a transition from his company TriRig. We talk about the basics of mediation, meditation in sport and how it’s helping athletes, along with his new company and their flagship product the OM Chair. Listen to this episode so you can quiet your mind and become faster.

item.title: Airwaav, Respiration Rate, Cortisol, & Lactate For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Breathing is something we all must do to survive. It’s also a major part of performance. Today, on Faster we talk with Dr. Dena Garner about a product called Airwaav. Their patented mouthpiece is helping athletes lower their respiration rate, cortisol levels, and lactate levels to improve performance. I was initially skeptical about the product and I found out that Dena was also skeptical. It’s why she set out to prove it wrong before realizing she couldn’t. Listen to this episode to learn ab...
item.description:
Breathing is something we all must do to survive. It’s also a major part of performance. Today, on Faster we talk with Dr. Dena Garner about a product called Airwaav. Their patented mouthpiece is helping athletes lower their respiration rate, cortisol levels, and lactate levels to improve performance. I was initially skeptical about the product and I found out that Dena was also skeptical. It’s why she set out to prove it wrong before realizing she couldn’t. Listen to this episode to learn about Airwaav and the science behind lower your respiration rate so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 5-10 Watts

item.itunes:title: myWindsock, Course Analysis, CdA, Power, & Planning For Cyclists
item.title: myWindsock, Course Analysis, CdA, Power, & Planning For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Have you ever been to a race and wished that you could have previewed the course beforehand? Wouldn’t it be great to get a feel for a course when you’re not close? When an athlete has this advantage, they enter race day with more piece of mind. Ben Nobury at myWindsock has been obsessing about this idea for some time. myWindsock provides a descriptive look at a course based on weather data, and rider data to help you understand that the hill your about to climb normally has a head wind. This ...
item.description:
Have you ever been to a race and wished that you could have previewed the course beforehand? Wouldn’t it be great to get a feel for a course when you’re not close? When an athlete has this advantage, they enter race day with more piece of mind. Ben Nobury at myWindsock has been obsessing about this idea for some time. myWindsock provides a descriptive look at a course based on weather data, and rider data to help you understand that the hill your about to climb normally has a head wind. This data allows you the athlete to mentally prepare ahead of time for the course so you’re not adjusting on the fly. myWindsock also uses advanced algorithms to help you predict times on a course and tells you the right places to apply power for your race to optimize your time. Listen to this episode to learn how to cheat the wind and be prepare for your next event so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 15-30

item.itunes:title: Fuelin, Meal Planning, Gut Training, & Stomach Issues For Cyclists
item.title: Fuelin, Meal Planning, Gut Training, & Stomach Issues For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Following a diet plan can be challenging at best. What starts off as a perfect pdf with meals planned out meticulously, usually ends up causing more stress. What happens if your buddies want to hang out and grab dinner but the restaurant doesn’t have the Salmon and Quinoa your meal plan calls for? What if you get called out to a work dinner or simply haven’t been able to get to the grocery store due to your busy schedule? Today, on Faster we talk with Co-Founder Scott Tindal of Fuelin who’s m...
item.description:
Following a diet plan can be challenging at best. What starts off as a perfect pdf with meals planned out meticulously, usually ends up causing more stress. What happens if your buddies want to hang out and grab dinner but the restaurant doesn’t have the Salmon and Quinoa your meal plan calls for? What if you get called out to a work dinner or simply haven’t been able to get to the grocery store due to your busy schedule? Today, on Faster we talk with Co-Founder Scott Tindal of Fuelin who’s making performance nutrition plans for athletes easy and stress free. Scott years of experience working with professional athletes in triathlon, the Toronto Maple Leafs, and Team Oracle are now the brains behind the app Fuelin. Fuelin not only optimizes your meal plans for your season but also helps you train your gut so that you can consume the calories you need on race day. Stomach issues during a race are so common but Scott’s athletes are finding this is a thing of the past. Listen to this episode to learn about optimizing your nutrition with Fuelin’s AI so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 100

item.itunes:title: Cross Training For Cyclists
item.title: Cross Training For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Cross training has been viewed in two ways, One, as the best thing for athletes who are looking to perform at a high level in their sport and two, as time taken away from further developing that sport. When considering children’s sports, the topic is even more debated. Today on Faster we talk with Matt Kritz who specializes in strength training athletes in multiple disciplines. His time with the New Zealand Olympic program lead, him to develop a movement screen and a prescriptive way to get a...
item.description:
Cross training has been viewed in two ways, One, as the best thing for athletes who are looking to perform at a high level in their sport and two, as time taken away from further developing that sport. When considering children’s sports, the topic is even more debated. Today on Faster we talk with Matt Kritz who specializes in strength training athletes in multiple disciplines. His time with the New Zealand Olympic program lead, him to develop a movement screen and a prescriptive way to get athletes to progressively load their bodies with strength training to better prepare them for their sport. During that time, he’s also developed theories around cross training for athletes of all ages. Listen to this episode to learn what type of cross training can make you and your kids faster.

Watt Points: 50

We discuss Purple Patches theories on training, movement analysis, nutrition & hydration, strength training, an...

What if someone were to take a practical vs a technical approach to training? What if you looked at how to fit training into your life instead of how to fit life around your training? Could you train less and achieve more? Today on Faster we talk with well-known coach Matt Dixon from Purple Patch Fitness who delivers high-performance triathlon training for time starved athletes. We discuss Purple Patches theories on training, movement analysis, nutrition & hydration, strength training, and much more. Listen to this episode to learn how taking a balanced approach to life and training can make you faster.

item.itunes:title: Zero Friction Cycling, Chain Lube, Immersion Waxing, & Drive Trains For Cyclists
item.title: Zero Friction Cycling, Chain Lube, Immersion Waxing, & Drive Trains For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: There are several hundred thousand friction instances per minute in a chain when cycling. This creates a lot of friction and can waste up to 20 watts if your chain is not properly lubricated and maintained. Chain lubrication options have exploded over the last few years and many people find themselves confused when trying to understand what’s best for them. Unfortunately, there is also a lot of bad information around lubrication. Today on Faster we talk with Adam Kerin from Zero Friction Cycl...
item.description:
There are several hundred thousand friction instances per minute in a chain when cycling. This creates a lot of friction and can waste up to 20 watts if your chain is not properly lubricated and maintained. Chain lubrication options have exploded over the last few years and many people find themselves confused when trying to understand what’s best for them. Unfortunately, there is also a lot of bad information around lubrication. Today on Faster we talk with Adam Kerin from Zero Friction Cycling who is leading the way in cycling lubrication knowledge and testing. We discuss all of the lubrication types and why immersion waxing is best and not as difficult as you may think. Listen to this episode to learn about chain lubrication and how to save up to 30 watts in your next race.

Watt Points: 10-30
item.content:encoded:
There are several hundred thousand friction instances per minute in a chain when cycling. This creates a lot of friction and can waste up to 20 watts if your chain is not properly lubricated and maintained. Chain lubrication options have exploded over the last few years and many people find themselves confused when trying to understand what’s best for them. Unfortunately, there is also a lot of bad information around lubrication. Today on Faster we talk with Adam Kerin from Zero Friction Cycling who is leading the way in cycling lubrication knowledge and testing. We discuss all of the lubrication types and why immersion waxing is best and not as difficult as you may think. Listen to this episode to learn about chain lubrication and how to save up to 30 watts in your next race.

Watt Points: 10-30

item.title: GiBLI, Aero Sensors, & On Road Testing For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: On road aero testing has been taking off over the last few years. We’ve worked with several sensors and can attest to the fact that they can be challenge to use and understand. Today on Faster we talk with GiBLI who is aiming to make their on road aero sensor as easy to use as a power meter. Their advance sensors and intuitive UI is making that possible. Listen to this episode to learn how a GiBLI sensor can help you save 30-80 watts so you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Understanding FatMax, Energy Systems, & The Lactic System
item.title: Understanding FatMax, Energy Systems, & The Lactic System
item.itunes:summary: During our podcast on muscle composition a question was posed on what happens to the body when an athlete becomes fat adapted. That led us down a rabbit hole with two previous guests from UNLV, Dr. John Mercer and Tedd Girouard. In this episode we uncover the mystery behind FatMax, aerobic and anaerobic processes and the energy they use, and talk about how lactate isn't the thing that makes your muscles burn and how it's a key to you getting faster. Listen to this episode to gain knowledge ab...
item.description: During our podcast on muscle composition a question was posed on what happens to the body when an athlete becomes fat adapted. That led us down a rabbit hole with two previous guests from UNLV, Dr. John Mercer and Tedd Girouard. In this episode we uncover the mystery behind FatMax, aerobic and anaerobic processes and the energy they use, and talk about how lactate isn't the thing that makes your muscles burn and how it's a key to you getting faster. Listen to this episode to gain knowledge about what your body is doing when you train and how to optimize your training to make the most of your body and the way it's designed to work.
item.content:encoded: During our podcast on muscle composition a question was posed on what happens to the body when an athlete becomes fat adapted. That led us down a rabbit hole with two previous guests from UNLV, Dr. John Mercer and Tedd Girouard. In this episode we uncover the mystery behind FatMax, aerobic and anaerobic processes and the energy they use, and talk about how lactate isn't the thing that makes your muscles burn and how it's a key to you getting faster. Listen to this episode to gain knowledge about what your body is doing when you train and how to optimize your training to make the most of your body and the way it's designed to work.

item.itunes:title: Macronutrients, Micronutrients, Carbs, Protein, Fat, & Vitamins For Cyclists
item.title: Macronutrients, Micronutrients, Carbs, Protein, Fat, & Vitamins For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Nutrition is what fuels our performances. In this episode we talk with pro cyclist and nutrition master Lori Nedescu about macronutrients and micronutrients and what roles they play in the body for cyclist. We break down the macronutrients protein, carbohydrates, and fat and then discuss the types of micronutrients, water soluble vitamins, fat soluble vitamins, and minerals. Listen to this episode to learn how to optimize your macronutrients and micronutrients to become faster.

item.title: Mike Reilly The Voice Of Ironman & Inspiration For Triathletes
item.itunes:summary: Someone’s voice can be a very powerful thing. For Mike Reilly, his voice has inspired and changed the lives of many. Mike is known as “The Voice of Ironman” and has brought more then 400,000 athlete’s across the line. This gives him one of the most unique views of what makes someone faster. We sit down with Mike to discuss what defines an Ironman, his tips for being faster, inspiring stories he’s seen over the years, the evolution of triathlon, giving back and his new book. item.title: Muscle Composition, Fast Twitch, Slow Twitch, Fiber Types For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Muscles are a key component in cycling. Without them we’d get nowhere. You may have heard of fast twitch and slow twitch muscle fiber but that’s just the surface of what really going on inside the body. Today, on faster we talk with Mike Schultz from Highland Training about muscle energy systems, muscle composition, how to train specific muscle fibers, and common muscles issues. Listen to this episode to understand how your muscle work and how to train them so that you can become faster. Wa...
item.description:
Muscles are a key component in cycling. Without them we’d get nowhere. You may have heard of fast twitch and slow twitch muscle fiber but that’s just the surface of what really going on inside the body. Today, on faster we talk with Mike Schultz from Highland Training about muscle energy systems, muscle composition, how to train specific muscle fibers, and common muscles issues. Listen to this episode to understand how your muscle work and how to train them so that you can become faster.

Watt Points: 30 Watts

Listen to this episode to understand how your muscle work and how to train them so that you can become faster.

item.itunes:title: Triathlon, Group Training, & Community For Cyclists
item.title: Triathlon, Group Training, & Community For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Triathlon is known as a solo sport. When you’re racing it’s you against yourself and whatever the race throws at you. But getting to the race is a different story. Patrick McCrann from Endurance Nation build a business around a community of athletes that train together, to encourage each other and ultimately get faster. Patrick is a 10-time Kona Ironman athlete with a 9:20 under his belt and also an INSCYD coach with a very successful track record training others. Listen to the episode to lea...
item.description:
Triathlon is known as a solo sport. When you’re racing it’s you against yourself and whatever the race throws at you. But getting to the race is a different story. Patrick McCrann from Endurance Nation build a business around a community of athletes that train together, to encourage each other and ultimately get faster. Patrick is a 10-time Kona Ironman athlete with a 9:20 under his belt and also an INSCYD coach with a very successful track record training others. Listen to the episode to learn how training with a community will help you become a smarter and faster athlete.

Watt Points: 30

In this episode we discuss all things wheel selection from rules of thumbs, myths, disciplines, gender, height & weight, psychology, wind speed, and much more. We discuss tire selection & sizing, why FLO wheels have been optimized for rolling resistance, pressure management, and more. We also discuss how FLO has studied and contributed to rolling resistance and some of the things we are working on here to learn more. Listen to Part 3 of our mini series to learn about rolling resistance and how you can optimize it to make you a smarter and FASTER athlete.

item.description:
In Part 2 of our mini series we discuss aerodynamics. We build your knowledge from the ground up and discuss terms commonly used such as drag, yaw angles, yaw torque, and relative velocity. Next we walk you through our approach to aerodynamics in wheel design and discuss a number of things we've learned that you can use to make your wheels faster. We finish the episode with aerodynamic advice for all things cycling related to you can optimize yourself to be as aero as possible. Listen to Part 2 of our mini series to learn how FLO can help make you FASTER with aerodynamics.

Watt Points: 50-100

item.itunes:title: Dan Lorang, Training Blocks, Inscyd, & Ironman For Cyclists
item.title: Dan Lorang, Training Blocks, Inscyd, & Ironman For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: A few episodes ago, we spoke with Sebastian from Inscyd on the amazing work they are doing with metrics and sports science. Today, on FASTER we talk with legendary coach Dan Lorang about coaching using Inscyd metrics vs. a standard FTP plan, how he assesses athletes, typical training blocks, and the athlete coach relationship. Dan is the long-time coach of Jan Frodeno & Anne Haug and he became the first coach in history to have simultaneous male and female Ironman Kona World Champions in ...
item.description:
A few episodes ago, we spoke with Sebastian from Inscyd on the amazing work they are doing with metrics and sports science. Today, on FASTER we talk with legendary coach Dan Lorang about coaching using Inscyd metrics vs. a standard FTP plan, how he assesses athletes, typical training blocks, and the athlete coach relationship. Dan is the long-time coach of Jan Frodeno & Anne Haug and he became the first coach in history to have simultaneous male and female Ironman Kona World Champions in 2019. If that’s not enough, Dan is also the Head of Performance and Coaching for Team Bora with riders like Peter Sagan and Pascal Ackermann. Listen to this episode if you want to think about coaching using metrics other than FTP and how to find your best coach so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 30
item.content:encoded:
A few episodes ago, we spoke with Sebastian from Inscyd on the amazing work they are doing with metrics and sports science. Today, on FASTER we talk with legendary coach Dan Lorang about coaching using Inscyd metrics vs. a standard FTP plan, how he assesses athletes, typical training blocks, and the athlete coach relationship. Dan is the long-time coach of Jan Frodeno & Anne Haug and he became the first coach in history to have simultaneous male and female Ironman Kona World Champions in 2019. If that’s not enough, Dan is also the Head of Performance and Coaching for Team Bora with riders like Peter Sagan and Pascal Ackermann. Listen to this episode if you want to think about coaching using metrics other than FTP and how to find your best coach so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 30

item.itunes:title: Efficient Pedaling, Cadence, & Crank Arm Length For Cyclists
item.title: Efficient Pedaling, Cadence, & Crank Arm Length For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Pedaling— it’s something most of us learn at a young age and we don’t give it much thought. Our legs seem to know how to do it. The perfect pedal stroke is often debated in cycling- crank arm length, optimal cadence, and much more. In this episode, we speak with Dr. Jim Martin from the University of Utah. Jim’s career has focused on pedaling and he dispels some of the age old rumors floating around the cycling community. Pedaling is something we are wired to do and giving it too much thought ...
item.description:
Pedaling— it’s something most of us learn at a young age and we don’t give it much thought. Our legs seem to know how to do it. The perfect pedal stroke is often debated in cycling- crank arm length, optimal cadence, and much more. In this episode, we speak with Dr. Jim Martin from the University of Utah. Jim’s career has focused on pedaling and he dispels some of the age old rumors floating around the cycling community. Pedaling is something we are wired to do and giving it too much thought actually causes us to slow down. Listen to this episode and learn about pedaling efficiency, metabolic cost, and what role our joints play in powering our pedals, so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 0-60

item.itunes:title: Bio-Neurofeedback For Cyclists
item.title: Bio-Neurofeedback For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Bio-Neurofeedback is one of the most fascinating topics I’ve heard of in recent years. To be completely honest, until I researched and understood the work it was difficult to believe. The human brain is a complex organ that produces five frequencies of brain waves produced from the electrical pulses between neurons. What scientists are learning, is that we can control what frequencies our brains produce and from a sport performance perspective, optimize our brain waves to compete and win at t...
item.description:
Bio-Neurofeedback is one of the most fascinating topics I’ve heard of in recent years. To be completely honest, until I researched and understood the work it was difficult to believe. The human brain is a complex organ that produces five frequencies of brain waves produced from the electrical pulses between neurons. What scientists are learning, is that we can control what frequencies our brains produce and from a sport performance perspective, optimize our brain waves to compete and win at the highest level. Today, on FASTER, we speak with Dr. Penny Werther from the University of Calgary about her work on bio-neurofeedback with the Canadian Olympic team that has led to numerous gold medals. Listen to this episode to learn how to calm your mind and narrow you focus so you can become faster.

Watt Points: Not Enough Research To Know
item.content:encoded:

item.itunes:summary: Chiropractic care is often thought of as bone popping and joint manipulation. If you’re like me, a bad first experience may have turned you off of the idea of chiropractic care as an effective treatment. Today on faster we talk with Dr. Kyle Buth who’s convinced me that chiropractic care is incredibly valuable, and his neurological approach to medicine has changed my life along with many others. If you’re looking to stay healthy and at the top of your game, listen to this episode to learn abo...
item.description:
Chiropractic care is often thought of as bone popping and joint manipulation. If you’re like me, a bad first experience may have turned you off of the idea of chiropractic care as an effective treatment. Today on faster we talk with Dr. Kyle Buth who’s convinced me that chiropractic care is incredibly valuable, and his neurological approach to medicine has changed my life along with many others. If you’re looking to stay healthy and at the top of your game, listen to this episode to learn about how the body perceives and manages pain, and the ways it can be treated so you can become faster.

Watt Points: 0-300

item.itunes:title: Light Therapy, Recovery, Inflammation, & ATP For Cyclists
item.title: Light Therapy, Recovery, Inflammation, & ATP For Cyclists
item.itunes:summary: Light Therapy, when I first heard of it a year ago I thought it was a crazy idea. However, my opinion has changed. Today on Faster, we discuss light therapy with Wes Pfiffner of Joovv. We cover light fundamentals, and how red, IR, and NIR light are improving performance, recovery, and lowering inflammation by increasing ATP production in our cells. Our modern lives leave us indoors 93% of time which gives us an abundance of blue light and lack of red, IR, and NIR light. Listen to this episode...
item.description:
Light Therapy, when I first heard of it a year ago I thought it was a crazy idea. However, my opinion has changed. Today on Faster, we discuss light therapy with Wes Pfiffner of Joovv. We cover light fundamentals, and how red, IR, and NIR light are improving performance, recovery, and lowering inflammation by increasing ATP production in our cells. Our modern lives leave us indoors 93% of time which gives us an abundance of blue light and lack of red, IR, and NIR light. Listen to this episode to learn how light can improve your overall health and make you faster.  

Watt Points: 15-20
item.content:encoded:

Our modern lives leave us indoors 93% of time which gives us an abundance of blue light and lack of red, IR, and NIR light.
